REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, PETITIONER MARTIN MUNETON'S MOTION FOR WRITS OF ADMINISTRATIVE MANDAMUS CCP 1094.5 AND ORDINARY MANDAMUS CCP 1085 IS DENIED. THE HEARING DECISION DIRECTLY CONTRADICTS HUD REGULATIONS. FURTHER, PETITIONER DOES NOT SHOW THAT SFHA HAS A MINISTERIAL DUTY TO FOLLOW THE DECISIONS OF ITS HEARING OFFICERS. THE RELATED DECLARATORY RELIEF REQUESTED IS THEREFORE DENIED. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312(B). JUDGE: CHARLES F. HAINES; CLERK: J. MURPHY; NOT REPORTED. (D501)

calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion And Motion For Writs Of Administrative Mandamus Ccp 1094.5 And Ordinary Mandamus Ccp 1085
Off Calendar Apr-12-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCSES:
Patricia Shnell, 415.701.1200 of Aids Legal Referral Panel appearing via Zoom for Petitioner Martin Muneton.
Thomas McCarrick, 415.788.1900 of Murphy, Pearson, Bradley & Feeney appearing via Zoom for Respondent San Francisco Housing Authority.

Hearing held. Petitioner contests. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows: PETITIONER MARTIN MUNETON's Motion For Writs Of Administrative Mandamus Ccp 1094.5 And Ordinary Mandamus Ccp 1085 is DENIED. The hearing decision directly contradicts HUD regulations. Further, Petitioner does not show that SFHA has a ministerial duty to follow the decisions of its hearing officers. The related declaratory relief requested is therefore denied. Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: J. Murphy; Not Reported. (D501)
